title: Esthetician

rationales.taskAutomation: Skincare treatments require hands-on technique and licensed judgment about skin conditions; only intake and basic product recommendations are automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI skin-analysis apps (imaging-based diagnostics) are a real and improving product category used in consultations.
rationales.adoption: A growing share of spas and dermatology-adjacent practices use AI skin analysis for intake, but treatment delivery itself is untouched.
rationales.agenticExposure: Client intake, skin analysis, and product recommendation are agent-ownable; the physical treatment is not.

tasksAtRisk: AI-assisted skin analysis and product matching
tasksGrowing: Hands-on treatment technique for facials and peels
Judgment on contraindications and skin sensitivities
Building client trust for ongoing skincare plans
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-5094, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: AI skin-analysis and diagnostic tool fluency

insight: AI imaging can now flag skin concerns and suggest a regimen during intake, but delivering the actual treatment, and judging contraindications on a real client, still requires a licensed, hands-on professional.
